Ceratochloa species, commonly known as downy brome, are annual grasses belonging to the family Poaceae. Ceratochloa species have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Ceratochloa species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to their medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ceratochloa species are often prepared as teas or infusions, as well as dried and processed into capsules or tinctures, although specific preparations may vary depending on the region and cultural context.
taxonomy: Ceratochloa brevis is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Ceratochloa, which contains several species of grasses. Ceratochloa brevis is native to North America and is commonly found in prairies and dry areas. The species is also known as short wind grass, due to its short stature and ability to thrive in windy conditions. Ceratochloa brevis is a perennial grass that grows in dense clumps and produces small, wind-pollinated flowers.
